title: MORPHOLOGICAL FEATURES OF DIABETES-INDUCED CARDIAC ISCHEMIA
author: Dilnoza Kurbanova  Dildora Sobirova
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The aim of this study was to evaluate the morphological changes that develop in heart and lung tissues in rabbits with alloxan-induced diabetes and myocardial ischemia modeled by left anterior descending (LAD) coronary artery ligation. In the experiment, 24 rabbits were divided into control, diabetes, myocardial ischemia, and combined (diabetes + ischemia) groups. Diabetes was induced by intraperitoneal administration of alloxan monohydrate (150 mg/kg), and ischemia was created by ligation of the LAD artery. Histological examinations were performed using hematoxylin-eosin, Masson trichrome, and PAS-reaction.  
  The results showed that cardiomyocyte hypertrophy, microangiopathic changes, and interstitial fibrosis developed in the diabetic group, while necrotic and degenerative processes prevailed in the ischemic group. In the combined model, the most severe structural disorders, capillary narrowing, and perfusion deficiency were observed in myocardial and lung tissues.  
  In conclusion, diabetes mellitus increases the susceptibility of the myocardium to ischemic injury and enhances microangiopathic and fibrotic processes. This model is an effective experimental system for studying the pathogenesis of cardiometabolic pathology.
